title: ceratosanthes rupicola
intro: Ceratosanthes rupicola, also known as rock vine or mountain snail vine, is a perennial vine native to the tropical regions of the Western Ghats in India. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Ceratosanthes rupicola include its ability to treat various health issues such as skin infections, wounds, and fever, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Ceratosanthes rupicola include infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which can be prepared by steeping the plant's leaves and stems in hot water or mixing them with other herbs to create a medicinal paste.
